name: Cascada del Guazalamanco - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
name_original: Cascada del Guazalamanco - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Hiking around Pozo Alcón offers access to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features a diverse topography with rugged mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and dense forests of black pines. Notable natural features include impressive karst formations, an extensive hydrological network with rivers like the Guadalentín, and the Embalse de la Bolera reservoir. High peaks such as Pico Gilillo (1,848 meters) provide panoramic views across the landscape. Best hiking trails in Pozo Alcón The most popular hiking route is Cascada del Guazalamanco - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Enchanted forest las Higueras - circular from Pozo Alcón, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail leads through the unique "Enchanted Forest of Higueras." Local hikers also love the Puente de la Herradura – Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Hotel Rural Dehesa del Rincón, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and past waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Pozo Alcón is defined by high peaks, karst formations, and extensive river networks, including the Guadalentín River and Embalse de la Bolera. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Pozo Alcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from around 120 reviews. More than 590 hikers have used komoot to explore Pozo Alcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Pozo Alcón?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Pozo Alcón, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, Pozo Alcón offers several easy trails perfect for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17266344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Spring Area in the Fig Forest – Guadalentín River at La Bolera loop from Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km long and takes about 1 hour 50 minutes. The region also features "The Enchanted Forest of Higueras" trail, designed for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Pozo Alcón?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Pozo Alcón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e982980163/cascada-del-guazalamanco-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Guazalamanco - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route.

Q: Are there trails that feature waterfalls in the Pozo Alcón area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in waterways and waterfalls. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33737365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura – Guazalamanco Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls to visit include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some of the highest peaks I can hike to in the Sierras de Cazorla near Pozo Alcón?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park offers challenging ascents to peaks exceeding 2,000 meters. You can hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is the highest point in Sierra de Cazorla at 1,848 meters, offering panoramic views. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañas Peak&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached via the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artesón Spring – Cabañas Peak loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pozo Alcón?
A: Pozo Alcón offers hiking opportunities year-round, but the best seasons are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural park is vibrant with flora or autumn colors. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring snow to the higher peaks.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced hiking routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region provides routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artesón Spring – Cabañas Peak loop from Cerro Gallego&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.6 km trail with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, leading to Cabañas Peak.

Q: What natural landmarks can I explore around Pozo Alcón besides the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, Pozo Alcón is surrounded by impressive natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Virgin of Tíscar and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a unique spot. The Embalse de la Bolera reservoir is also a beautiful natural area. The entire region is characterized by dramatic karst formations, deep valleys, and dense black pine forests.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Pozo Alcón?
A: The hiking routes in Pozo Alcón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 118 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Sierras de Cazorla, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near hiking routes in Pozo Alcón?
A: The extensive hydrological network, including the Guadalentín River and the Embalse de la Bolera, provides natural spots where you might find opportunities for a refreshing dip. Always check local regulations and safety conditions before swimming in natural waters.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach trailheads in Pozo Alcón?
A: While Pozo Alcón is well-situated within the natural park, public transport options directly to specific trailheads can be limited. It's often recommended to have personal transportation to access the more remote starting points for hikes. Check local bus schedules for connections to Pozo Alcón town itself.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Pozo Alcón?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and within the town of Pozo Alcón. For specific routes, look for designated parking areas at the start of the trails, especially near visitor centers or well-known access points to the natural park. Always park responsibly and respect local signage.
tips: Set of small waterfalls that form the Guazalamanco waterfalls, located on the river of the same name. After a rainy season, the waterfalls offer a very beautiful natural spectacle.
